9b7473cf-54b1-447a-80af-b680329329ef.body: To see and embrace Jesus Christ in all.

f227a626-0a7b-46f2-9bf7-4e57fc4a4b1c.body: To see and embrace Jesus Christ in all.

0e9255eb-7143-46c5-8723-0085c4d9fa5f.body: This is bigger than Saturday morning. We are talking about all 168 hours of the week, in the joys and the sufferings, the triumphs and the tragedies, the conversations that make you think and the ones that leave you sitting quietly for a while. When we say 'all,' we mean it. Nothing is outside the reach of what God is doing, and we want to help people see that. Not as a program. As a way of living.

ca9878c1-989f-4417-a3ee-a8256fffbb77.body: We are building toward a Redlands where the people who have been told church is not for them find out that's just not true. Not because we softened anything, but because we made room for their questions, their doubts, and their whole complicated lives. The picture of success for us is not a bigger room on Saturday. It is a city where more people are becoming more loving, and some of that started here.

e6b77a08-ec2f-434d-aa40-ae5009fef3da.body: Intellectual Honesty: We do not ask anyone to check their mind at the door. We would not do it ourselves.

7a71fb94-177a-4d7f-8571-03555e97590e.body: Compassion Over Correctness: We would rather be known for love than for being right.

62f1e457-294f-4c16-b3d3-5cf1551ca414.body: Radical Welcome: Open arms, open minds, open hearts. That is the standard, not the slogan.

1af5bda6-3941-48f6-9499-6df472646943.body: Curiosity as Worship: Questions are not a sign of weak faith. They are what living faith actually looks like.

5748d66d-9cc5-45c0-8ab5-38e04d6f8da6.body: Theological Humility: Nobody here has a monopoly on God. We mean that.

7dc9144e-e70d-4d18-bbb2-fd95b4a185e3.body: Arms wide open to inclusion, minds wide open to intellect, and hearts wide open to compassion.

12366154-df87-4ed2-a1dc-a17ab9906e45.body: Sermons at Paradox are designed to start discussions, rather than end them.

beb52b25-2f03-4a91-b0cb-d5667caad76f.body: Spiritual maturity requires us to embrace the paradoxes we face, rather than resolve them. God is fully human and fully divine. In the Easter story, God dies and God lives.

4f113570-37f3-4436-aa05-c481d9d24777.body: The Only Place in Redlands Where Progressive Thinking and Christian Tradition Meet Without Either One Being Watered Down

96869154-f04a-4d11-b5a7-a05e2494eb42.body: What makes us genuinely different is not that we are progressive, and it is not that we are traditional. It is that we refuse to choose. Most communities that hold progressive values quietly set the tradition aside to get there. And most communities that hold the tradition treat progressive questions as a threat. We hold both, not as a tension to manage but as a paradox to inhabit.

c27c9b51-731f-4e67-bfaa-e1c1cdbe0383.body: While we are currently a non-denominational church, Paradox grew out of the Seventh-day Adventist Christian tradition. When we started the church in 2016, we had a strong desire to keep our founding community together, which was a group that met on Saturday mornings.

91782a73-ab95-4624-a929-00515b77a790.body: We only ask for money once every week, during the church service, because money is of little importance to us.

cc47d307-545e-4d09-942c-43a78b287754.body: We don't use any language that references sacrificial atonement, because we view God's love as a gift, not an obligation.

8e9a08b8-7030-40a2-a478-dbb3eb6430da.body: We don't use any language that talks about 'Our God' or 'True religion', because God is God of all, and every religion on earth has something of value to add to their perspective of Divine.

83a89d7d-2e77-4b9e-9ca7-942b6b9b40a9.body: Avoid empty religious language. We don't mind using religious language, as long as we understand what it means. We also don't like to just quote Bible verses for the sake of quoting them.

4a67d00b-bdca-459e-90ab-7aed77ddd42e.body: Write from alongside, not from above. Use 'we' and 'us' language that puts the writer in the journey with the reader.

67b2a05b-25a2-4b25-ad38-43b37b54b551.body: Avoid confident declarations about what God is doing. Stay curious and humble. It is okay to name the hard thing and not resolve it.

7a884751-1e81-48ad-a98a-517ad86c9790.body: Reference the texture of the idea, not just the idea itself. Discovery language lands better than curriculum language. Let intellectual content feel like finding something, not being taught something.

be43f59d-8138-4b35-9c6d-81006776d0ad.body: Let delight show up in the details. Do not force enthusiasm. Our joy is real — write from that real place.

0796049f-290c-4920-b6c4-eecacb9f746e.body: Reference the richness of the tradition without gatekeeping it. The tradition is a resource for transformation, not a destination to defend.

0bbb24ed-aa89-4c18-801b-0625f2abd77d.body: Do not write your way out of tension. Name it and let it breathe. Phrases like 'we are still figuring this out' and 'we do not have a clean answer to that' are assets, not weaknesses.

9d8abec5-d910-417b-b516-173c32c043d2.body: Make invitations specific, not vague. 'Come join us' says nothing. 'Come spend an hour thinking about something that actually matters' says something. The invitation at Paradox is to a conversation, not a service.

138b4066-4ca4-4455-999a-896fcd96b655.body: Top voice attribute: Conversational. Supporting: Disarming, Honest, Passionate.

ea5ae651-5164-4ea9-a3b3-526e7028894b.body: Top cultural attribute: Intellectually Engaged. Supporting: Honest and Open, Joyful, Radically Welcoming.

a867fd6b-66dd-4826-857a-3319ac061954.body: Top feeling attribute: Wonder. Supporting: Hopeful, Curious Disbelief, Emotionally Moved.

a16101e9-9d81-415a-a25e-64f2a1487c8e.body: Desired emotions: Gratitude, Hope, and Wonder.

10001c0f-5b08-4827-869e-83aa2d60ce13.body: Gracious, inclusive, loving, and filled with wonder. The language should be the same across all platforms (online, on stage, on camera).

3a51b669-0afe-4e00-93c9-6e175ec7a5ac.body: Top impact attribute: Love. Supporting: Transformation (becoming more loving), Permission to Question, Finding a Community Where You Belong.

8953be02-ac44-493d-86d8-8a7378f1c92d.body: Welcome to Paradox, an open and affirming church in Redlands where you can be part of the conversation about what it means to trust the love of God in our modern world.

b783c4ea-c63c-4c07-9f4c-84b631453645.body: Sermons at Paradox Start Discussions Rather than End Them. You don't have to agree with the Pastor to be part of Paradox. Instead, we hope these teachings spark conversations about how we can all become more loving people.

f4df97d8-8174-43f6-ae69-847ff9a20353.body: We are so glad you found us! The common tie between everyone here is that we all have a desire to become more loving people. If you share that same desire, we would love for you to join us!

392296a7-f9df-4504-9863-d352da819ee3.body: At Paradox, we create a welcoming, inclusive space where everyone can belong. Our services typically last about 75 minutes and include modern music, thought-provoking sermons, and reflective moments that are designed to start discussions rather than end them.

72aabd1a-1445-4bcf-accc-b07d3b044a98.body: Got questions or looking to dive in? Grab a coffee with our pastor, who loves to listen and help you find community. So many folks have walked away from these coffee chats feeling uplifted and more connected. Why not you too?

34073465-f3de-4130-9555-5d2aebd1b97d.body: Many people at Paradox are rethinking what they believe and why. You don't have to agree with the pastor or have everything figured out to belong here. Honest questions, uncertainty, and thoughtful conversation are part of the journey — and we're committed to exploring it together in a way that leads to love, growth, and deeper understanding.

ba1ecfda-801b-43f0-9a1e-7d7d3687175c.body: Our church is one of the few open and affirming churches that has a modern, contemporary style worship. You will be invited to sing along to atypical church songs which are meant to help you journey inward. We are certain you are going to disagree with something Pastor Craig says in his sermons. That is encouraged and okay! Diversity of thought is a gift from our Creator.

3f0e799b-6c45-4197-86ab-7c77218f641d.body: Our music is invitational, not required. Some people sing, some dance, some sit and reflect, some simply listen. You're free to engage in any way that feels authentic and comfortable to you.

9d24a2a6-2c1e-4308-af8a-f995ca9bec0f.body: We celebrate communion every week and all are welcome at the table of God. No matter what you believe, who you voted for, how you identify, or who you love, we would be honored if you chose to take communion with us.

bca6893b-119e-4374-a589-6160ce3cbcd4.body: We have found spiritual maturity requires all of us to embrace paradox and contradiction. For example, Jesus Christ is fully human AND fully divine. Jesus Christ lives AND, in the Gospel story, dies. And God is always found where God should not be. This name reminds us to embrace, rather than resolve, the paradoxes and contradictions we encounter.

84b45df8-b909-4d3f-9cd3-8c489b1bea3e.body: At Paradox, we fully affirm and celebrate the image of God in all queer persons. We officiate queer weddings, ordain queer clergy, baptize queer folks, and welcome people to serve in every level of leadership at our church with full appreciation of their gender identity and/or sexual orientation.

362ce76f-f310-45e6-b7db-259276b3f3e1.body: At Paradox, we believe every person's culture and religion is a unique and irreplaceable testament to the Divine. We invite speakers from a wide variety of backgrounds to teach us about God from the wisdom of their own sacred traditions, beautiful histories, and personal experiences.

f6d58578-573f-4f14-ba3b-99d8e1c1e17e.body: At Paradox, we strive to empower and celebrate women. Half of our Elder board is female (including our Head Elder), we ordain female pastors, we believe the feminine perspective is necessary to understand the divine, and we frequently use feminine pronouns to describe our infinite Creator.

5fb61cf1-51f9-447d-982f-3b105ee6a44a.body: Our church exists to help you see and embrace Jesus Christ in all 168 hours of the week (rather than just one hour during a church service).

b41e2aae-8632-44cf-90db-e36ad2c8880e.body: Today, our community continues to cherish both ancient traditions of Christianity and the modern movement of the Holy Spirit— calling us into new realities, deeper love, and more expansive understanding every day. This ongoing exploration of both tradition and transformation is just one of the reasons we call ourselves Paradox.

6abeb149-b07b-4272-8a32-554b39286e67.body: At the heart of our community lies a foundation built on the life, death, and resurrection of Jesus Christ. His teachings, principles, and way of life guide our actions, shape our teachings, and inspire us to become more loving people.

7ca8aa39-6aa7-494b-baf7-58bfa8e3afb1.body: No matter where you are in your spiritual journey, you are welcome here. In our Young Adults group, we build deep relationships, spend time together, and discover that your spiritual journey is worthy of exploration.

71c671e2-2a57-4bb4-b4d5-49d4429b813f.body: Many of us are re-examining what we believe and why – giving ourselves permission to question the ideas, traditions, and assumptions we inherited. Together, we walk through what still feels true, what no longer fits, and how our beliefs can shape us into more loving, grounded, and whole people. You don't need answers to belong here – just curiosity and willingness to grow alongside others.

5de183e1-846f-4a65-b587-a6a531b1b2d8.body: Sermons at Paradox are designed to start conversations, not end them. Our discussion groups give you space to process, ask questions, and grow in community.

a5ad009b-6b64-4c39-85e2-6ec4567756b1.body: If you are new to church, deconstructing religion, or reconstructing faith, then you can find a spiritual home at Paradox Church.

bf6099bc-7675-4048-96b0-8ee1c3ed0e39.body: Paradox Kids creates a joyful, welcoming space where children ages 5–9 can explore faith through creativity, curiosity, and hands-on involvement.

73a9658c-2a73-472f-aeed-f9b21cb70532.body: We want all of our kids to grow up with a sense that God's love is generous, inclusive, and affirming of all human beings.

5b6ad9e0-afd7-406c-a8de-2bb6921a9a12.body: Paradox isn't just for adults — it's for kids too! Paradox Youth gives 10-14 year olds a space that feels like theirs: they get to hang out with their friends, share snacks, create fun projects, and talk honestly about life, faith, and who God created them to be.

090ac567-db38-4665-998e-e2e1cde28644.body: Actions Speak Louder Than Words — make a tangible difference in the here and now.

570d09e1-9b3a-496b-9e2e-95ad3d8866dd.body: Embrace Wonder, Beauty, Joy, Meaning, Hope, Christ in Everything

ae548f15-382a-4d6a-8b89-be3fe9d2065b.body: Start Discussions, Rather Than End Them

e4cb2c24-3bce-4311-b82e-e56c34ceb165.body: helping children see GOD IN EVERYTHING

94df26ac-175e-45d7-b447-29a2ebbd91d2.body: before you were even born, god loved you first

2528ca66-ceca-47ee-80ff-a3d06bb58fb9.body: BEGINNING WITH A STRONG SENSE OF BELONGING

5270d801-8318-408e-8b41-4938baf554a0.body: making paradox church their own — where faith, friendship, and fun meet

1f1a49b9-2e13-4cab-8b3a-897c78e23e7c.body: Making sense of faith in the modern world

9f2abd91-7c95-41e8-a45a-032707805ab0.body: God invites all of us to become more loving people. So how are you challenging yourself to trust love in this week ahead?

9f09f0d9-e4e0-419d-a4a7-8ea82fbbc36b.body: We believe that God can be found in all that we encounter. The joys and the sufferings. The laughter and the tears. The triumphs and the tragedies. Nothing is beyond the redemption of God.

32f9abe8-a64a-4a55-b7ed-6617be944086.body: We completed a nine-year, sixty-six part sermon series through every book of the Bible in 2022 (we started before we were officially a church). We liked it so much, that we started doing it again in 2024. We are currently on part 11 of this series (The Gospel of Matthew).

81aee0e3-74d2-4f12-a405-d305e1a373d4.body: Our Holy Saturday service (the day before Easter Sunday), we discussed the space between the death of Friday and the life of Sunday. We used Sleeping at Last songs to illustrate grief, loss, and hope in light of all of it. It was one of the best church services we have put on.

9cdda142-528a-4a64-947d-b665a3a22f51.body: Value Proposition: At Paradox, you do not have to choose between your intellect and your faith, and you do not have to have your doubts resolved before you belong. We are an open and affirming community in Redlands that takes scripture seriously, asks hard questions honestly, and measures success not by what you believe but by how you love.

91d1ed0e-2e05-42a9-b9cc-5747d220ebe1.body: Plan a Visit page must do heavy lifting for first-time visitors: answer the unasked questions (What is the service actually like? What time? Where do I park? What happens with my kids? Is this really a safe place for my family?). Current version is functional but the WordPress build should make this page a genuine on-ramp.

0f5dfda6-7212-44f8-8904-516baac48de3.body: Staff and team pages need a WordPress staff management solution that Craig or Amelia can update easily without touching code or rebuilding a section. Current Squarespace grid is hand-placed images requiring manual updates during ongoing staff transitions.

62acff61-7fda-412f-831a-f12783b2fd18.body: Discussion guides should be consolidated into a single, easy-to-update location on WordPress — currently managed in multiple places.

effe4be8-632e-4c01-873c-6388bb7d6c14.body: Sermon library and blog: Craig wants weekly sermon-based blog posts generated and published as part of the content strategy. The site needs to be structured so this content is easy to add, well-organized, and SEO-friendly. Blog and sermon archive should feel like a natural extension of the teaching, not an afterthought.

2c6fd4eb-8556-4d7d-855a-ed7a19394593.body: The affirming identity messaging needs to be confident and specific without feeling like a political statement. It should feel like a natural expression of who we are, not a disclaimer.

95d826c0-49ed-4c2a-8c25-9784a9cd2168.body: Full stories with character arcs. Our sermons are longer for this reason.

eafa7caa-1c8a-4ca3-baa6-7f9f08617daa.body: Our church LOVES historical context, academic research, the scientific method, and literary analysis. All of it can lead us into the presence of God.

09cc46e9-5156-48a7-a869-cbea3019d747.body: Brand Statement: We are a church for people who take their questions as seriously as their faith, built on the conviction that loving like Jesus is the whole point.

9bc2c1cc-d4c8-4146-be72-9f3a44d80c25.body: We sound like we're in it with you. Warm, curious, and honest — offering real depth in plain language. Thoughtful without being academic — so people don't just learn something; they feel less alone.

doc.purpose: Single source-of-truth that the cowork pipeline reads when picking templates + populating slots. Bind-time translation between this uniform vocabulary and Brixies field names lives in the app-side import endpoint, NOT in cowork.

cowork_vocabulary.primary_heading: text, REQUIRED, page-section title (Brixies h1/h2 depending on template)
cowork_vocabulary.body: richtext, optional, descriptive prose under the heading
cowork_vocabulary.accent_body: richtext, optional, secondary descriptive prose. Only on templates that have a SECOND visible richtext block.
cowork_vocabulary.buttons: array of { label, url } CTAs. max_items varies per template.
cowork_vocabulary.items: array of { item_heading, item_body, item_meta? }. Used by accordion, cards, tabs, timeline, team, etc. max_items + palette ref vary per template; the importer figures out the visual layout.

doc.designer_slots: Every image slot in the underlying Brixies template is designer-populated (not by cowork). The total per template is recorded as design_handoff_image_count for the design handoff checklist.
importer_responsibilities: Translate uniform_to_brixies field names for the picked template
For multi-group templates (accordion_faq, content_image_text_b, contact_section): split items[] across the visual groups per layout-specific rule
For palette-ref groups (feature_team, content_featured_a): look up the project curated_library.card_* binding to resolve the actual Card variant + write item fields against that variant
Validate every required uniform slot is populated before INSERT into web_sections
Leave all image fields empty (placeholders render until designer fills via design handoff)

empty_slot_prevention.philosophy: Required slots empty at bind time = preventable upstream. Every layer validates against this manifest BEFORE handing off.
layers: plan-cross-page-allocation: for each section_intent, check that allocated sources contain content sufficient to fill the picked template required slots. Gaps land in unresolved_sources with exact slot named.
outline-page: declares atom_assignments covering all required uniform slots before returning. Self-validates against the manifest.
draft-page: validates its own draft output before returning. Every required slot has non-empty content. One retry on gap. Else returns validation.unresolved_required_slots[].
importer (app-side): last line of defense. Refuses INSERT if any required slot empty. Returns structured error pointing at page + section + slot + missing atom + suggested fix.
Strategist UI: surfaces unresolved gaps with context. Strategist sees exactly which page/section/slot needs input, what was missing, what to do.
